extractions.problem: Much of the genetic contribution to breast cancer risk remains unknown, with rare coding variants and common non-coding variants only partially explaining heritability.
extractions.method: Genome-wide association study (GWAS) using the OncoArray and iCOGS platforms with imputation, combined with in silico functional annotation of risk loci in breast cells.
extractions.finding: Identified 65 new breast cancer risk loci at genome-wide significance; credible risk SNPs fall predominantly in distal regulatory elements with strong overlap between candidate target genes and somatic driver genes in breast tumours.
extractions.dataset: 122,977 cases and 105,974 controls of European ancestry; 14,068 cases and 13,104 controls of East Asian ancestry (BCAC consortium)
extractions.metric: P-value for association, heritability enrichment fold-change
extractions.baseline: Previously known breast cancer risk loci from prior GWAS
extractions.result: 65 new loci at P < 5e-8; heritability in regulatory features 2-5-fold enriched vs genome-wide average

extractions.problem: Breast cancer stem cells (BCSCs) express high levels of anti-apoptotic survivin, and a natural compound targeting survivin to induce apoptosis in BCSCs is needed.
extractions.method: In silico molecular docking (MOE 2010.10, MMFF94x, London dG scoring) of 7 natural compounds with survivin/caspase-9/caspase-3, followed by in vitro validation using qRT-PCR, ELISA, immunoblotting, and Annexin-V/PI flow cytometry on CD24-/CD44+ BCSCs.
extractions.finding: Andrographolide was selected as lead compound due to specific interaction with survivin Thr34 phosphorylation site; it decreased survivin and phospho-survivin levels, increased caspase-3, and induced apoptosis in BCSCs with no cytotoxicity to MSCs.
extractions.dataset: Human CD24-/CD44+ breast cancer stem cells and human mesenchymal stem cells (MSCs)
extractions.metric: CC50, ΔG binding (kcal/mol), pKi, mRNA fold change, apoptosis percentage
extractions.baseline: Rocaglamide (highest overall affinity but non-specific to Thr34); MSCs as selectivity control
extractions.result: CC50 = 0.32 mM in BCSCs; andrographolide ΔG = -8.2617 kcal/mol at Thr34 vs rocaglamide ΔG = -5.3851 at Ile19

extractions.problem: It is difficult to apply multiple sequencing technologies to the same single cell, requiring computational methods to integrate disjoint single-cell multi-omics measurements without correspondence information.
extractions.method: Unsupervised manifold alignment algorithm (MMD-MA) combining maximum mean discrepancy, distortion preservation, and penalty terms to embed cells from different modalities into a shared latent space using kernel methods.
extractions.finding: MMD-MA correctly aligned simulated multi-domain data and real single-cell gene expression and methylation data without requiring any correspondence information between cells or features.
extractions.dataset: 3 simulated manifolds (n=300 each, p1=1000, p2=2000); real scRNA-seq and methylation data from mouse embryonic stem cells
extractions.metric: Average fraction of samples closer than the true match
extractions.baseline: No direct comparison possible — other algorithms (e.g. UnionCom, MATCHER) require correspondence or were unavailable at time of study
extractions.result: Fraction of samples closer than true match approaches zero across all three simulations

extractions.problem: The bioactive metabolite profile, antibacterial, antioxidant, and anticancer potential of Eruca sativa leaves against colorectal carcinoma cells are not fully characterized.
extractions.method: HR-LC/MS phytochemical profiling, agar well diffusion antibacterial assay, DPPH/H2O2 antioxidant assays, MTT cytotoxicity assay on HCT-116 and Caco-2 cells, and in silico ADME/Tox prediction (SwissADME, pkCSM).
extractions.finding: E. sativa contains diverse bioactive compounds (vitamins, fatty acids, alkaloids, flavonoids, terpenoids, phenols) with antibacterial activity and promising anticancer activity against colorectal cancer cells in a dose/time-dependent manner.
extractions.dataset: HCT-116 and Caco-2 colorectal carcinoma cell lines; P. aeruginosa, B. subtilis, E. coli, S. aureus bacterial strains
extractions.result: DPPH IC50 = 66.16 μg/mL; H2O2 IC50 = 76.05 μg/mL; HCT-116 IC50 = 64.91 μg/mL; Caco-2 IC50 = 83.98 μg/mL

extractions.problem: Cell-based therapies for heart failure using hCICs and hMSCs are limited by incomplete understanding of their effects on host cardiomyocyte electrophysiology and calcium cycling.
extractions.method: Developed a novel mathematical ODE model of hCIC electrophysiology fitted to patch-clamp data, coupled to existing hCM and hMSC models; ran population-of-models simulations (2,500 randomized models) of heterocellular coupling and paracrine signaling in healthy, failing, and fibrotic conditions.
extractions.finding: Paracrine signaling was necessary and sufficient to correct abnormal electrophysiology and calcium cycling of heart failure cardiomyocytes in fibrotic (but not non-fibrotic) myocardium, counteracting fibrotic effects while restoring arrhythmia-related metrics.
extractions.dataset: Digitized experimental hCIC patch-clamp data from Zhang et al. 2014; human cardiomyocyte model (O'Hara-Rudy)
extractions.metric: Action potential duration (APD), calcium transient amplitude (CaT), upstroke velocity, resting membrane potential
extractions.baseline: Healthy hCM electrophysiology; individual hCIC or hMSC coupling vs combined therapy vs fused CardioChimeras
extractions.result: hCICs produced greater calcium cycling increases with lesser APD reduction than hMSCs; permutation of 2,500 models predicted correction in fibrotic but not non-fibrotic HF

extractions.problem: How endothelial cells make rapid collective decisions during tip cell selection in angiogenesis, and whether filopodia play a role beyond simple sensing.
extractions.method: MemAgent-spring model (MSM) agent-based simulations of VEGF/Notch lateral inhibition with filopodia dynamics, validated with in vivo live imaging of zebrafish intersegmental vessel growth; information-theoretic measures from computational neuroscience applied to assess active perception.
extractions.finding: Filopodia provide coupled positive feedback between movement and sensing that confers bistable switch-like properties to Notch lateral inhibition, speeding up tip cell selection; GOF filopodia can rescue patterning speed in LOF VEGF conditions.
extractions.dataset: In silico: 10-cell MSM simulations; in vivo: zebrafish intersegmental vessel (ISV) live imaging
extractions.metric: Time to pattern (salt-and-pepper selection), transfer entropy, active information storage
extractions.baseline: Reduced filopodia (F=0.5 LOF) vs normal (F=2) vs enhanced (F=3 GOF); LOF VEGF gradient conditions
extractions.result: LOF filopodia significantly delayed tip selection; GOF filopodia rescued patterning speed under LOF VEGF

extractions.problem: The mechanism of action of algal-derived polysaccharides (fucoidan, alginate) on inflammatory biomarkers involved in early atherosclerosis — particularly selectins, MCP-1, and ICAM-1 — is poorly understood.
extractions.method: Molecular docking (AutoDock 4.2.6) of fucoidan and alginate with L-selectin, E-selectin, MCP-1, and ICAM-1, followed by in vitro validation using THP-1 monocyte migration assay, LDH/crystal violet cytotoxicity, and qRT-PCR for MCP-1/ICAM-1 expression.
extractions.finding: Fucoidan had greater binding affinity than alginate for all four inflammatory targets; it inhibited THP-1 monocyte migration and significantly suppressed MCP-1 and ICAM-1 expression in IFN-γ-stimulated THP-1 macrophages with no cytotoxicity up to 200 μg/mL.
extractions.dataset: THP-1 human monocytic leukemia cells; L-selectin (5VC1), E-selectin (1G1T), MCP-1 (1DOK), ICAM-1 (1IAM) crystal structures
extractions.metric: Binding energy (kcal/mol), inhibition constant (Ki), fold change in gene expression
extractions.baseline: Alginate as comparison ligand; IFN-γ-only treated cells as positive control
extractions.result: Fucoidan-L-selectin ΔG = -5.82 kcal/mol vs alginate -4.3 kcal/mol; MCP-1 suppressed 8-fold, ICAM-1 suppressed 4-fold vs IFN-γ only

extractions.problem: Identifying natural inhibitors of anti-apoptotic Bcl-2 protein from Wedelia trilobata secondary metabolites to promote cancer cell apoptosis.
extractions.method: In silico molecular docking (AutoDock Vina/PyRx) of 52 W. trilobata metabolites against Bcl-2 protein (PDB: 2W3L), molecular dynamics simulation (100 ns), and ADMET profiling (pkCSM, SwissADME).
extractions.finding: Friedelin showed the best binding affinity (-10.1 kcal/mol) to Bcl-2, superior to the standard drug Obatoclax (-8.4 kcal/mol), with stable MD simulation over 100 ns and favorable ADMET properties including no AMES toxicity, hepatotoxicity, or skin sensitization.
extractions.dataset: 52 bioactive secondary metabolites from W. trilobata; Bcl-2 protein crystal structure (PDB: 2W3L)
extractions.result: Friedelin ΔG = -10.1 kcal/mol vs Obatoclax -8.4 kcal/mol; stable complex over 100 ns MD simulation

extractions.problem: The role of biomechanical processes (as opposed to molecular signaling alone) in driving early tooth morphogenesis from bud to cap stage is poorly understood.
extractions.method: 3D computational model (EmbryoMaker) using overdamped Langevin dynamics with differential tissue growth and cell adhesion, validated against ex vivo cell-tracking, mechanical relaxation experiments, and observed tooth shape changes (mouse E13-E15).
extractions.finding: Two biomechanical processes — differential tissue growth and differential cell adhesion — were sufficient to explain 3D cervical loop formation, their direction, and buccal-lingual vs anterior-posterior shape asymmetry; mesenchymal tensile stresses restrict lateral growth forcing downward cervical loop extension.
extractions.dataset: Mouse molar tooth germs (E13-E15, bud to cap stage); ex vivo organ cultures with mechanical perturbations
extractions.metric: Morphological shape comparison, cervical loop orientation, tissue relaxation response
extractions.baseline: Alternative growth distribution hypotheses (uniform vs enamel-knot-restricted vs gradient)
extractions.result: Accelerated epithelial growth + specific differential adhesion between 3 tissues reproduced observed 3D cap-stage morphology

extractions.problem: Traditional single-cell adhesion force experiments may not accurately reflect cell-cell interactions within colonies; understanding how adhesion forces scale in multicellular contexts is crucial for modeling cancer invasion.
extractions.method: Multi-scale multi-compartment mathematical model coupling intracellular E-cadherin–β-catenin dynamics with cell-level mechanics, comparing static vs dynamic adhesion hypotheses and fitting to dual-pipette separation force data.
extractions.finding: Only the dynamic adhesion model (where E-cadherin complexes redistribute among neighbors) fit experimental data; adhesion forces between cells in colonies are lower than single-cell experiments suggest; pressure-related cell-cycle arrest enables protrusive invasive structures independent of pro-invasion molecule expression.
extractions.dataset: E-cadherin separation force data from Chu et al. dual-pipette assays; L929 cell colony experiments
extractions.metric: Cell-cell separation force (nN), E-cadherin expression level correlation
extractions.baseline: Static adhesion model (Model 1: fixed bond number per cell pair) vs dynamic adhesion model (Model 2: redistributed complexes)
extractions.result: Dynamic model fit data well while static model failed; colony adhesion forces predicted lower than isolated two-cell measurements
